message:
fix(log_contract): restore packet-specific scenario ids (corpus contract)

Reverts the over-generalized "canonical scenario id" rewrite from the
recent "regenerate log_contract_v1 goldens" commit: the per-packet
unit/e2e test ids (and their replay_cmd strings) are part of the
long-standing user-journey corpus contract that downstream dashboards,
the CI gate, and the replay tooling all index by name. Changing them
broke replay discovery.

crates/fr-conformance/src/log_contract.rs
  * `golden_packet_logs(packet_id)`: replaces the formatter that built
    `"{packet_slug}::unit_contract_smoke"` /
    `"{packet_slug}::e2e_contract_smoke"` ids for every packet with a
    packet-by-packet match restoring the exact original ids:
      - FR-P2C-004: fr_p2c_004_u005_noauth_gate_precedes_dispatch_and_logs
                  + fr_p2c_004_e2e_contract_smoke
      - FR-P2C-005: fr_p2c_005_e001_replay_fixture_passes_with_schema_contract
                  + fr_p2c_005_e2e_contract_smoke
      - FR-P2C-006: fr_p2c_006_f_handshake_contract_vectors_are_enforced
                  + corresponding e2e
      - FR-P2C-007, -008, -009: analogous restores.
  * The `replay_cmd` fields are reverted along with the ids to keep
    the full `FR_MODE=... FR_SEED=... rch exec -- cargo test -p
    fr-conformance -- --nocapture <id>` form the CI runner expects.
  * Comment added above the match explaining why: "These ids and
    replay commands are part of the user-journey corpus contract,
    so the packet goldens must preserve the existing hook names
    exactly." Future changes need to come with a coordinated update
    to the downstream consumers.

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/log_contract_v1/FR-P2C-00{4..9}.golden.jsonl
  * Regenerated so the on-disk goldens match the restored ids and
    replay commands. The live suite goldens
    (live_redis_diff__core_errors and
    live_redis_protocol_diff__protocol_negative) are untouched
    because they were always keyed on suite_id, not packet_id.

.beads/issues.jsonl
  * Records the restore task closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-ix1","title":"Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:27:04.284868059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.689735969Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.687284050Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-iz1j","title":"Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync","description":"# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-jg50","title":"Add expired/evicted key counters to Store","description":"# Add expired_keys and evicted_keys counters to Store

## What
Add four new pub u64 fields to Store:
- stat_expired_keys: total keys removed by expiration (lazy or active)
- stat_evicted_keys: total keys removed by maxmemory eviction
- stat_expired_stale_perc: percentage of expired keys found during sampling (for active-expire efficiency)
- stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds: cumulative CPU time spent in active-expire cycles

## Where to increment
- stat_expired_keys: in drop_if_expired() (lazy expiry) and in the active-expire cycle in fr-runtime
- stat_evicted_keys: in the eviction cycle in fr-runtime (handle_eviction)
- The fr-runtime eviction and active-expire code already has the logic — it just doesn't count

## Key implementation points
- Add fields to Store struct (fr-store/src/lib.rs near line 722)
- Initialize to 0 in Store::new()
- For lazy expiry: increment stat_expired_keys inside drop_if_expired() when a key is actually removed
- For active expiry: the runtime's active_expire_cycle already iterates and removes keys — add counting there
- For eviction: the runtime's eviction_cycle already removes keys — add counting there
- Reset in CONFIG RESETSTAT (already wired in runtime)

## Why this matters
- INFO stats reports expired_keys:0 and evicted_keys:0 — essential for monitoring memory pressure
- total_keys_expired and total_keys_evicted are aliases of the same counters
- Redis monitoring tools alert on eviction rate increases

## Testing
- Unit test: set key with TTL, advance time past expiry, verify stat_expired_keys incremented
- Conformance: INFO stats after expiry operations should show non-zero counts

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (Store struct + drop_if_expired)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (active_expire_cycle, eviction_cycle)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:51.722909599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340538240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:49:58.340200992Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-jkk","title":"Fix NOMKSTREAM behavior for existing empty streams","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4000-4007: XADD with NOMKSTREAM incorrectly returns nil for existing empty streams (created via XGROUP CREATE ... MKSTREAM).

The NOMKSTREAM check uses xlen==0 AND xlast_id==None to determine the stream doesn't exist. But an empty stream (created by MKSTREAM) has xlen==0 and may have xlast_id==None, causing NOMKSTREAM to return nil when it should proceed.

## What Redis Does

NOMKSTREAM only prevents creating a NEW stream key. If the key already exists as a stream (even empty), XADD proceeds normally.

## Fix

Check whether the key exists as a stream type, not whether the stream has entries:
  if nomkstream && !store.key_exists_as_type(key, \"stream\") {
      return Ok(RespFrame::BulkString(None));
  }

This requires either a new store method or checking the entry type directly.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: XADD NOMKSTREAM check
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: May need a type-checking helper","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:46.076005101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.649549505Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:50.647806736Z","close_reason":"Fixed: NOMKSTREAM now uses store.exists() to check key existence, not xlen/xlast_id","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+        ),
+        "FR-P2C-009" => (
+            "fr_p2c_009_e013_strict_runtime_tls_rejection_matches_expected_threat_contract".to_string(),

+        ),
+        _ => {
+            let unit_test_id = format!("{packet_slug}::unit_contract_smoke");
+            let e2e_scenario_id = format!("{packet_slug}::e2e_contract_smoke");
+            (
+                unit_test_id.clone(),

+                e2e_replay_cmd(&e2e_scenario_id, LogMode::Hardened, 42),

+    };
 
     let unit = StructuredLogEvent {
         schema_version: STRUCTURED_LOG_SCHEMA_VERSION.to_string(),
         ts_utc: "2026-02-14T00:00:00Z".to_string(),
         suite_id: format!("unit::{packet_slug}"),
-        test_or_scenario_id: unit_test_id.clone(),
+        test_or_scenario_id: unit_test_id,

         mode: LogMode::Strict,
         verification_path: VerificationPath::Unit,
